NAME

session - Session

Detailed Description

This header provides a thin wrapper around the lib/audio/ system for session-level audio coordination. It handles the differences between host (mixing) and participant (simple capture/playback) modes.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Unified API for both host and participant audio handling
  • Host-only audio mixing for multi-participant sessions
  • Source management for tracking participant audio streams
  • Clean startup/shutdown coordination

USAGE: 

// Create audio context for a participant
session_audio_ctx_t *ctx = session_audio_create(false);
// Start capture and playback
session_audio_start_capture(ctx);
session_audio_start_playback(ctx);
// Cleanup
session_audio_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides a unified interface for media capture that abstracts over webcam, file, stdin, and test pattern sources. It is designed to be reusable across different modes (client, mirror, and discovery mode).

CORE FEATURES:

  • Unified API for all media source types (webcam, file, stdin, test)
  • Configurable frame rate with adaptive sleep
  • Optional frame resizing for network transmission
  • FPS tracking and reporting
  • Loop support for file sources

USAGE: 

// Create capture context for webcam at 60 FPS
session_capture_config_t config = {


    .type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,


    .path = "0",


    .target_fps = 60,


    .loop = false,


    .resize_for_network = false
};
session_capture_ctx_t *ctx = session_capture_create(&config);
// Read and process frames
while (!done) {


    image_t *frame = session_capture_read_frame(ctx);


    if (frame) {


        // Process frame...


    }


    session_capture_sleep_for_fps(ctx);
}
// Cleanup
session_capture_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides a unified interface for terminal display that abstracts TTY detection, palette initialization, and frame rendering. It is designed to be reusable across different modes (client, mirror, and discovery mode).

CORE FEATURES:

  • TTY detection and management
  • Terminal capability detection (color, UTF-8)
  • Palette initialization and luminance mapping
  • Frame rendering with RLE expansion support
  • Snapshot mode support for single-frame capture

USAGE: 

// Create display context with color support
session_display_config_t config = {


    .snapshot_mode = false,


    .palette_type = PALETTE_STANDARD,


    .custom_palette = NULL,


    .color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO
};
session_display_ctx_t *ctx = session_display_create(&config);
// Render frames
const char *frame_data = ...; // ASCII frame from server
session_display_render_frame(ctx, frame_data, false);
// Cleanup
session_display_destroy(ctx);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides the server-side session hosting abstraction, encapsulating client management, connection acceptance, and event handling for session hosts.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Server lifecycle management (start, stop)
  • Client connection handling (add, remove, lookup)
  • Callback-based event notification
  • Encryption and authentication support

USAGE: 

// Define callbacks
void on_client_join(session_host_t *h, uint32_t id, void *data) {


    printf("Client %u joined\n", id);
}
void on_frame(session_host_t *h, uint32_t id, const image_t *frame, void *data) {


    // Process video frame from client
}
// Create and start host
session_host_config_t config = {


    .port = 27224,


    .max_clients = 32,


    .callbacks = { .on_client_join = on_client_join, .on_frame_received = on_frame }
};
session_host_t *h = session_host_create(&config);
session_host_start(h);
// Run until stopped...
// Cleanup
session_host_destroy(h);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Provides keyboard input handling for session-level controls including:

  • Media playback control (play/pause, seek)
  • Audio control (volume, mute)
  • Display control (color mode, webcam flip)
  • Help screen toggle

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides the client-side session participation abstraction, encapsulating connection management, media streaming, and event handling for session participants.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Connection lifecycle management (connect, disconnect, reconnect)
  • Video and audio stream control
  • Callback-based event notification
  • Encryption and authentication support

USAGE: 

// Define callbacks
void on_connected(session_participant_t *p, uint32_t id, void *data) {


    printf("Connected with ID %u\n", id);
}
void on_frame(session_participant_t *p, const char *frame, void *data) {


    // Render ASCII frame
}
// Create and connect
session_participant_config_t config = {


    .address = "127.0.0.1",


    .port = 27224,


    .callbacks = { .on_connected = on_connected, .on_frame_received = on_frame }
};
session_participant_t *p = session_participant_create(&config);
session_participant_connect(p);
// Start streaming
session_participant_start_video(p);
// Cleanup
session_participant_destroy(p);

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

This header provides settings structures and serialization functions for session configuration that can be transmitted between peers in discovery mode.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Compact binary serialization for network transmission
  • Version-based conflict detection
  • Bidirectional conversion with options system
  • Future-proof extensible structure with reserved bytes

USAGE: 

// Create settings from current options
session_settings_t settings;
session_settings_from_options(&settings);
// Serialize for transmission
uint8_t buffer[SESSION_SETTINGS_SERIALIZED_SIZE];
size_t len;
session_settings_serialize(&settings, buffer, &len);
// Deserialize received settings
session_settings_t received;
session_settings_deserialize(buffer, len, &received);
// Check if update needed
if (session_settings_needs_update(local_version, received.version)) {


    session_settings_apply_to_options(&received);
}

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

typedef struct session_audio_ctx session_audio_ctx_t

#include <src/common/session/audio.h>

Opaque session audio context handle. Manages audio capture, playback, and optional mixing state. Created via session_audio_create(), destroyed via session_audio_destroy().

Definition at line 56 of file src/common/session/audio.h.

typedef struct session_capture_ctx session_capture_ctx_t

#include <src/common/session/capture.h>

Opaque session capture context handle. Manages media source, FPS tracking, and adaptive sleep state. Created via session_capture_create(), destroyed via session_capture_destroy().

Definition at line 127 of file common/session/capture.h.

typedef image_t *(* session_capture_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/render.h>

Capture callback for event-driven frame source. Called to obtain the next frame for rendering. Can return NULL if no frame is currently available.

Used when capture context is NULL (event-driven mode). Not called if capture context is provided (synchronous mode).

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

Pointer to frame (caller must not free), or NULL if unavailable

Definition at line 59 of file common/session/render.h.

typedef bool(* session_capture_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/capture.h>

Callback type to check if initialization should be cancelled. Called periodically during initialization to allow graceful cancellation. Should return true if initialization should stop immediately.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel initialization, false to continue

Definition at line 71 of file common/session/capture.h.

#include <src/client/display.h>

Opaque session display context handle. Manages TTY state, terminal capabilities, palette, and rendering state. Created via session_display_create(), destroyed via session_display_destroy().

typedef struct session_display_ctx session_display_ctx_t

#include <src/common/session/display.c>

typedef bool(* session_display_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/display.h>

Callback type to check if initialization should be cancelled. Called periodically during initialization to allow graceful cancellation. Should return true if initialization should stop immediately.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel initialization, false to continue

typedef struct session_host session_host_t

#include <src/common/session/host.c>

Internal session host structure. Opaque session host handle.

Contains server state, client list, and callback configuration.

Manages server state, client connections, and event callbacks. Created via session_host_create(), destroyed via session_host_destroy().

typedef void(* session_keyboard_handler_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, int key, void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/render.h>

Keyboard input handler callback type. Called when keyboard input is detected during render loop iteration. Allows applications to respond to interactive keyboard controls.

Parameters

capture Capture context (may be NULL for client mode)
key Keyboard key code from keyboard_read_nonblocking()
user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Definition at line 87 of file common/session/render.h.

typedef struct session_participant session_participant_t

#include <src/common/session/participant.c>

Internal session participant structure. Opaque session participant handle.

Contains connection state, media stream state, and callback configuration.

Manages connection state, media streams, and event callbacks. Created via session_participant_create(), destroyed via session_participant_destroy().

typedef struct session_server_like_config session_server_like_config_t

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Configuration for session_server_like_run()

All logging, terminal, and signal settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the callbacks and user data below.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_init_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Mode-specific initialization callback.

Called once after shared setup (keepawake, logging) completes. Should create sockets, load keys, start worker threads—everything the mode needs before its main accept loop starts.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.init_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 68 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_interrupt_fn) (void)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Async-signal-safe interrupt callback.

Called from signal handler context (SIGINT / SIGTERM). Must only use async-signal-safe operations. Responsible for closing listening sockets to unblock the blocking run_fn (accept loop or poll).

Examples:

  • Server mode: close g_tcp_server listen sockets + websocket cancel
  • Discovery-Service mode: set g_server->tcp_server.running = false + acds_signal_exit()

Definition at line 98 of file server_like.h.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_server_like_run_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Mode-specific blocking main loop.

Called after init_fn succeeds. Blocks until the server shuts down. Typical implementations:

  • Server mode: tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server)
  • Discovery-Service mode: acds_server_run(&g_server)

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code (cleanup always runs)

Definition at line 83 of file server_like.h.

typedef void(* session_server_like_status_fn) (void *user_data, ui_status_t *out_status)

#include <src/common/session/server_like.h>Status screen data callback.

Called at GET_OPTION(fps) Hz by the status screen thread. Should fill *out_status with current state for ui_status_display(). If NULL, the status screen is not shown even if --status-screen is set.

The callback receives a zeroed ui_status_t and must populate ALL fields it wants displayed (mode_name, addresses, port, connected_count, session_string, start_time, etc.). No static fields are inherited from the config — status_fn owns the entire ui_status_t.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer from config.status_user_data
out_status Output struct to populate (pre-zeroed by caller, not NULL)

Definition at line 117 of file server_like.h.

typedef bool(* session_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/render.h>

Exit condition callback type. Called each iteration to check if render loop should terminate. Return true to exit, false to continue.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true if loop should exit, false to continue

Definition at line 43 of file common/session/render.h.

typedef void(* session_sleep_for_frame_fn) (void *user_data)

#include <src/common/session/render.h>

Sleep callback for custom frame timing. Called to sleep/wait until the next frame is ready. Allows custom timing logic.

Used when capture context is NULL (event-driven mode). Not called if capture context is provided (synchronous mode).

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

#include <src/common/session/audio.h>

Add an audio source for mixing (host only).

Parameters

ctx Audio context (must not be NULL, must be host context)
source_id Unique identifier for this source

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Registers a new audio source for the mixer. Each participant should have a unique source_id.

Note

Only available when context was created with is_host=true.

225                                                                                          {
226   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
227     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_audio_add_source: invalid context");
228   }
229 
230   if (!ctx->is_host) {
231     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_audio_add_source: not a host context");
232   }
233 
234   // Check if source already exists
235   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
236     if (ctx->sources[i].active && ctx->sources[i].source_id == source_id) {
237       return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Already registered
238     }
239   }
240 
241   // Find empty slot
242   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
243     if (!ctx->sources[i].active) {
244       ctx->sources[i].source_id = source_id;
245       ctx->sources[i].active = true;
246       ctx->sources[i].buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create();
247       if (!ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
248         ctx->sources[i].active = false;
249         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for source");
250       }
251       ctx->source_count++;
252       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
253     }
254   }
255 
256   return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum audio sources reached");
257 }

Creates and initializes a session audio context. Hosts get audio

Call session_audio_destroy() to free resources

ctx Audio context to destroy (can be NULL)


Stops all audio streams, cleans up mixers (if host), and releases

116   }
117 
118   // Cleanup host-specific resources
119   if (ctx->is_host) {
120     // Destroy source buffers
121     for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
122       if (ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
123         audio_ring_buffer_destroy(ctx->sources[i].buffer);
124         ctx->sources[i].buffer = NULL;
125       }
126     }
127 
128     // Free mix buffer
129     if (ctx->mix_buffer) {
130       SAFE_FREE(ctx->mix_buffer);
131     }
132   }
133 
134   // Destroy underlying audio context
135   audio_destroy(&ctx->audio_ctx);
136 
137   ctx->initialized = false;
138   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
139 }

size_t
  session_audio_mix_excluding (session_audio_ctx_t * ctx, uint32_t
  exclude_id, float * output, size_t num_samples)


#include <src/common/session/audio.h>


Mix all sources except one and return the result (host only).


Parameters


ctx Audio context (must not be NULL, must be host
  context)


exclude_id Source ID to exclude from mix (for echo prevention)


output Output buffer for mixed audio (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to mix


Returns


Number of samples actually mixed


Mixes audio from all registered sources except exclude_id. This
    allows sending each participant a mix of all other participants without
    their own audio (preventing echo).




Note


Only available when context was created with
  is_host=true.


Definition at line 301 of file
    src/common/session/audio.c.


301                                                                                                                      {
302   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !output || num_samples == 0) {
303     return 0;
304   }
305 
306   if (!ctx->is_host) {
307     return 0;
308   }
309 
310   // Initialize output to silence
311   memset(output, 0, num_samples * sizeof(float));
312 
313   // Ensure we have a mix buffer
314   if (!ctx->mix_buffer || num_samples > ctx->mix_buffer_size) {
315     return 0;
316   }
317 
318   int sources_mixed = 0;
319 
320   // Mix all active sources except the excluded one
321   for (int i = 0; i < SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES; i++) {
322     if (!ctx->sources[i].active || ctx->sources[i].source_id == exclude_id) {
323       continue;
324     }
325 
326     if (!ctx->sources[i].buffer) {
327       continue;
328     }
329 
330     // Read samples from this source
331     size_t samples_read = audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->sources[i].buffer, ctx->mix_buffer, num_samples);
332 
333     if (samples_read == 0) {
334       continue;
335     }
336 
337     // Add to output mix
338     for (size_t j = 0; j < samples_read; j++) {
339       output[j] += ctx->mix_buffer[j];
340     }
341 
342     sources_mixed++;
343   }
344 
345   // Apply simple clipping prevention if multiple sources were mixed
346   if (sources_mixed > 1) {
347     float scale = 1.0f / (float)sources_mixed;
348     for (size_t i = 0; i < num_samples; i++) {
349       output[i] *= scale;
350       // Soft clipping
351       if (output[i] > 1.0f) {
352         output[i] = 1.0f;
353       } else if (output[i] < -1.0f) {
354         output[i] = -1.0f;
355       }
356     }
357   }
358 
359   return num_samples;
360 }

    session_audio_ctx::initialized, session_audio_ctx::is_host,
    session_audio_ctx::mix_buffer,
    session_audio_ctx::mix_buffer_size, SESSION_AUDIO_MAX_SOURCES,
    session_audio_source_t::source_id, and
    session_audio_ctx::sources.

  session_capture_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Legacy function - creates either mirror or network capture based
    on options. Maintained for backwards compatibility. New code should use
    session_mirror_capture_create() or
    session_network_capture_create() directly.




Parameters


config Capture configuration

Capture context, or NULL on error

Use session_mirror_capture_create() or

Definition at line 193 of file
    common/session/capture.c.


193                                                                                       {
194   // Auto-create config from command-line options if NULL
195   session_capture_config_t auto_config = {0};
196   if (!config) {
197     // Auto-initialization from GET_OPTION() only works when options have been parsed
198     // Verify by checking if we can access options (would SET_ERRNO if not)
199     const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
200     bool media_from_stdin = GET_OPTION(media_from_stdin);
201 
202     if (media_file[0] != '\0') {
203       // File or stdin streaming
204       auto_config.type = media_from_stdin ? MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN : MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
205       auto_config.path = media_file;
206       auto_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop) && !media_from_stdin;
207     } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
208       // Test pattern mode
209       auto_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
210       auto_config.path = NULL;
211     } else {
212       // Webcam mode (default)
213       static char webcam_index_str[32];
214       unsigned int idx = GET_OPTION(webcam_index);
215       safe_snprintf(webcam_index_str, sizeof(webcam_index_str), "%u", idx);
216       auto_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
217       auto_config.path = webcam_index_str;
218       log_debug("Webcam mode: using device index %u", idx);
219     }
220 
221     // Default settings suitable for local display
222     auto_config.target_fps = 60;
223     auto_config.resize_for_network = false;
224     config = &auto_config;
225   }
226 
227   // Check if we should exit before starting initialization
228   if (config->should_exit_callback && config->should_exit_callback(config->callback_data)) {
229     return NULL;
230   }
231 
232   // Allocate context
233   session_capture_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_capture_ctx_t), session_capture_ctx_t *);
234 
235   // Store configuration
236   ctx->target_fps = config->target_fps > 0 ? config->target_fps : 60;
237   ctx->resize_for_network = config->resize_for_network;
238 
239   // Store audio configuration
240   ctx->audio_enabled = config->enable_audio;
241   ctx->audio_fallback_enabled = config->audio_fallback_to_mic;
242   ctx->mic_audio_ctx = config->mic_audio_ctx;
243   ctx->using_file_audio = false;
244   ctx->file_has_audio = false;
245 
246   // Create media source from config type and path
247   ctx->source = media_source_create(config->type, config->path);
248 
249   if (!ctx->source) {
250     // Preserve existing error if set, otherwise set generic error
251     asciichat_error_t existing_error = GET_ERRNO();
252     if (existing_error == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
253       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to create media source");
254     }
255     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
256     return NULL;
257   }
258 
259   // Set up exit callback on media source for graceful shutdown during I/O
260   // This allows Ctrl+C to abort blocking FFmpeg calls like YouTube HTTP requests
261   if (config->should_exit_callback && ctx->source) {
262     media_source_set_exit_callback(ctx->source, config->should_exit_callback, config->callback_data);
263   }
264 
265   // Detect if media source has audio
266   if (ctx->audio_enabled && ctx->source) {
267     ctx->file_has_audio = media_source_has_audio(ctx->source);
268     if (ctx->file_has_audio) {
269       ctx->using_file_audio = true;
270       log_info("Audio capture enabled: using file audio");
271     } else if (ctx->audio_fallback_enabled && ctx->mic_audio_ctx) {
272       ctx->using_file_audio = false;
273       log_info("Audio capture enabled: file has no audio, using microphone fallback");
274     } else {
275       ctx->audio_enabled = false;
276       log_debug("Audio capture disabled: no file audio and no fallback configured");
277     }
278   }
279 
280   // Enable loop if requested (only for file sources)
281   if (config->loop && config->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
282     media_source_set_loop(ctx->source, true);
283   }
284 
285   // Set initial pause state if requested (only for file sources)
286   // Note: We don't pause yet - we'll pause AFTER reading the first frame in the render loop
287   // This is because paused sources return NULL from read_video()
288   if (config->type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && GET_OPTION(pause)) {
289     ctx->should_pause_after_first_frame = true;
290     log_debug("Will pause after first frame (--pause flag)");
291   }
292 
293   // Perform initial seek if requested
294   if (config->initial_seek_timestamp > 0.0) {
295     log_debug("Seeking to %.2f seconds", config->initial_seek_timestamp);
296     asciichat_error_t seek_err = media_source_seek(ctx->source, config->initial_seek_timestamp);
297     if (seek_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
298       log_warn("Failed to seek to %.2f seconds: %d", config->initial_seek_timestamp, seek_err);
299       // Continue anyway - seeking is best-effort
300     } else {
301       log_debug("Successfully seeked to %.2f seconds", config->initial_seek_timestamp);
302       // Codec buffers are flushed during seek, so next frame read will use correct position
303 
304       // Reset timing state after seek to prevent FPS calculation confusion
305       // (frame_count and elapsed_time must match the new playback position)
306       ctx->frame_count = 0;
307       ctx->start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
308 
309       // For snapshot mode with immediate snapshot (delay=0), we need to wait for prefetch thread to
310       // decode the seeked frame. This is a workaround for a timing issue where the prefetch thread
311       // needs time to decode the frame at the new position after seeking.
312       // The sleep duration must be long enough for the prefetch thread to decode one frame.
313       float snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
314       if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && snapshot_delay == 0.0f) {
315         // HTTP streams are slow - need 1+ second for first frame decode after seek
316         // Local files are faster - 100-200ms is usually sufficient
317         // Use 1 second as a safe default to handle both cases
318         log_debug("Waiting for prefetch thread after seek (snapshot_delay=0, HTTP streams need ~1 second)");
319         platform_sleep_us(US_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second - ensures prefetch thread has delivered seeked frame
320       }
321     }
322   }
323 
324   // Initialize adaptive sleep for frame rate limiting
325   // Calculate baseline sleep time in nanoseconds from target FPS
326   uint64_t baseline_sleep_ns = NS_PER_SEC_INT / ctx->target_fps;
327   adaptive_sleep_config_t sleep_config = {
328       .baseline_sleep_ns = baseline_sleep_ns,
329       .min_speed_multiplier = 0.5, // Allow slowing down to 50% of baseline
330       .max_speed_multiplier = 2.0, // Allow speeding up to 200% of baseline
331       .speedup_rate = 0.1,         // Adapt by 10% per frame if possible
332       .slowdown_rate = 0.1         // Adapt by 10% per frame if possible
333   };
334   adaptive_sleep_init(&ctx->sleep_state, &sleep_config);
335 
336   // Initialize FPS tracker
337   // Note: Must allocate tracker_name on heap since fps_init stores a pointer to it
338   char *tracker_name = SAFE_MALLOC(32, char *);
339   if (!tracker_name) {
340     media_source_destroy(ctx->source);
341     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
342     return NULL;
343   }
344   safe_snprintf(tracker_name, 32, "CAPTURE_%u", ctx->target_fps);
345   fps_init(&ctx->fps_tracker, (int)ctx->target_fps, tracker_name);
346 
347   // Record start time for FPS calculation (nanoseconds)
348   ctx->start_time_ns = time_get_ns();
349 
350   ctx->initialized = true;
351   return ctx;
352 }






References adaptive_sleep_init(),
    session_capture_ctx::audio_enabled,
    session_capture_ctx::audio_fallback_enabled,
    session_capture_config_t::audio_fallback_to_mic,
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio,
    session_capture_ctx::file_has_audio, fps_init(),
    session_capture_ctx::fps_tracker,
    session_capture_ctx::frame_count,
    session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,

double
  session_capture_get_current_fps (session_capture_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Get the current FPS being achieved by the capture source.

Returns

490   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || ctx->frame_count == 0) {
491     return 0.0;
492   }
493 
494   // Calculate elapsed time in nanoseconds then convert to seconds
495   uint64_t elapsed_ns = time_elapsed_ns(ctx->start_time_ns, time_get_ns());
496   double elapsed_sec = time_ns_to_s(elapsed_ns);
497 
498   if (elapsed_sec <= 0.0) {
499     return 0.0;
500   }
501 
502   return (double)ctx->frame_count / elapsed_sec;
503 }

  * session_capture_process_for_transmission (session_capture_ctx_t *
  ctx, image_t * frame)


#include <src/common/session/capture.h>


Process a frame for network transmission (resize if needed).


Parameters


ctx Capture context (must not be NULL)


frame Input frame from
  session_capture_read_frame() (must not be NULL)


Returns


Processed image ready for transmission, or NULL on
  error


Resizes the frame to network-optimal dimensions if
    resize_for_network was enabled in the configuration. The returned image is a
    new allocation owned by the caller and must be freed with
    image_destroy().




Note


Returns a copy if no resizing is needed.



Caller owns the returned image and must call
    image_destroy().




Definition at line 421 of file
    common/session/capture.c.


421                                                                                               {
422   if (!ctx || !frame) {
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_capture_process_for_transmission: NULL parameter");
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   // If resize is not enabled, just create a copy
428   if (!ctx->resize_for_network) {
429     image_t *copy = image_new(frame->w, frame->h);
430     if (!copy) {
431       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate image copy");
432       return NULL;
433     }
434     memcpy(copy->pixels, frame->pixels, (size_t)frame->w * (size_t)frame->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
435     return copy;
436   }
437 
438   // Calculate optimal dimensions for network transmission
439   ssize_t resized_width, resized_height;
440   calculate_optimal_dimensions(frame->w, frame->h, SESSION_MAX_FRAME_WIDTH, SESSION_MAX_FRAME_HEIGHT, &resized_width,
441                                &resized_height);
442 
443   // Check if resizing is actually needed
444   if (frame->w == resized_width && frame->h == resized_height) {
445     // No resizing needed - create a copy
446     image_t *copy = image_new(frame->w, frame->h);
447     if (!copy) {
448       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate image copy");
449       return NULL;
450     }
451     memcpy(copy->pixels, frame->pixels, (size_t)frame->w * (size_t)frame->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
452     return copy;
453   }
454 
455   // Create new image for resized frame
456   image_t *resized = image_new(resized_width, resized_height);
457   if (!resized) {
458     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate resized image buffer");
459     return NULL;
460   }
461 
462   // Perform resizing operation
463   image_resize(frame, resized);
464 
465   return resized;
466 }

Read the next video frame from the capture source.

Returns


Pointer to image frame, or NULL on error/end


Reads the next video frame from the media source. The returned

379                                                                 {

381     return NULL;
382   }
383 
384   static uint64_t last_frame_time_ns = 0;
385   uint64_t frame_request_time_ns = time_get_ns();
386 
387   image_t *frame = media_source_read_video(ctx->source);
388 
389   if (frame) {
390     uint64_t frame_available_time_ns = time_get_ns();
391 
392     // Track frame for FPS reporting
393     fps_frame_ns(&ctx->fps_tracker, frame_available_time_ns, "frame captured");
394     ctx->frame_count++;
395 
396     // Log frame-to-frame timing
397     if (last_frame_time_ns > 0) {
398       uint64_t time_since_last_frame_ns = time_elapsed_ns(last_frame_time_ns, frame_request_time_ns);
399       uint64_t time_to_get_frame_ns = time_elapsed_ns(frame_request_time_ns, frame_available_time_ns);
400       double since_last_ms = (double)time_since_last_frame_ns / NS_PER_MS;
401       double to_get_ms = (double)time_to_get_frame_ns / NS_PER_MS;
402 
403       if (ctx->frame_count % 30 == 0) {
404         log_dev_every(3 * US_PER_SEC_INT, "FRAME_TIMING[%lu]: since_last=%.1f ms, to_get=%.1f ms", ctx->frame_count,
405                       since_last_ms, to_get_ms);
406       }
407     }
408     last_frame_time_ns = frame_available_time_ns;
409 
410     // Handle --pause flag: pause after first frame is read
411     if (ctx->should_pause_after_first_frame && !ctx->paused_after_first_frame) {
412       media_source_pause(ctx->source);
413       ctx->paused_after_first_frame = true;
414       log_info("Paused (--pause flag)");
415     }
416   }
417 
418   return frame;
419 }

Returns

Converts the given image to ASCII art using:


    
  
  • Palette and terminal capabilities from display context
    • 
  
  • Width, height, stretch, and aspect ratio settings from GET_OPTION()

    callers don't need to manage palette, terminal capabilities, or conversion
    options.

    src/common/session/display.c.

495     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: ctx is NULL");
496     return NULL;
497   }
498

500     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: ctx not initialized");
501     return NULL;
502   }
503 
504   if (!image) {
505     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session_display_convert_to_ascii: image is NULL");
506     return NULL;
507   }
508 
509   // Get conversion parameters from command-line options
510   unsigned short int width = GET_OPTION(width);
511   unsigned short int height = GET_OPTION(height);
512   bool stretch = GET_OPTION(stretch);
513   bool preserve_aspect_ratio = !stretch;
514 
515   // Determine if we should apply flip_x and flip_y
516   bool flip_x_enabled = GET_OPTION(flip_x);
517   bool flip_y_enabled = GET_OPTION(flip_y);
518 
519   color_filter_t color_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
520 
521   // Handle dynamic matrix rain effect toggle
522   bool matrix_rain_enabled = GET_OPTION(matrix_rain);
523   if (matrix_rain_enabled && !ctx->digital_rain) {
524     // Initialize digital rain if it's now enabled but wasn't before
525     unsigned short int width_us = terminal_get_effective_width();
526     unsigned short int height_us = terminal_get_effective_height();
527     int width_for_rain = (int)width_us;
528     int height_for_rain = (int)height_us;
529     ctx->digital_rain = digital_rain_init(width_for_rain, height_for_rain);
530     if (ctx->digital_rain) {
531       digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, color_filter);
532       log_info("Matrix rain effect: enabled");
533     }
534   } else if (!matrix_rain_enabled && ctx->digital_rain) {
535     // Disable digital rain if it's now disabled but was enabled before
536     digital_rain_destroy(ctx->digital_rain);
537     ctx->digital_rain = NULL;
538     log_info("Matrix rain effect: disabled");
539   }
540 
541   // Make a mutable copy of terminal capabilities for ascii_convert_with_capabilities
542   terminal_capabilities_t caps_copy = ctx->caps;
543 
544   // Re-evaluate render_mode on every frame to pick up live changes
545   caps_copy.render_mode = (render_mode_t)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
546 
547   // MEASURE EVERY OPERATION - Debug systematic timing
548   uint64_t t_flip_start = time_get_ns();
549 
550   // Apply horizontal and/or vertical flips if requested
551   image_t *flipped_image = NULL;
552   const image_t *display_image = image;
553 
554   if ((flip_x_enabled || flip_y_enabled) && image->w > 1 && image->h > 1 && image->pixels) {
555     START_TIMER("image_flip");
556     uint64_t t_flip_alloc_start = time_get_ns();
557     flipped_image = image_new((size_t)image->w, (size_t)image->h);
558     uint64_t t_flip_alloc_end = time_get_ns();
559 
560     if (flipped_image) {
561       uint64_t t_flip_memcpy_start = time_get_ns();
562       // OPTIMIZATION: Copy entire image first (sequential memory access - cache-friendly)
563       memcpy(flipped_image->pixels, image->pixels, (size_t)image->w * (size_t)image->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
564       uint64_t t_flip_memcpy_end = time_get_ns();
565 
566       uint64_t t_flip_reverse_start = time_get_ns();
567 
568       // Apply horizontal flip (X-axis)
569       if (flip_x_enabled) {
570 #if SIMD_SUPPORT_NEON
571         // Use NEON-accelerated flip on ARM processors
572         image_flip_horizontal_neon(flipped_image);
573 #else
574         // Scalar fallback for non-NEON systems
575         for (int y = 0; y < image->h; y++) {
576           rgb_pixel_t *row = &flipped_image->pixels[y * image->w];
577           for (int x = 0; x < image->w / 2; x++) {
578             rgb_pixel_t temp = row[x];
579             row[x] = row[image->w - 1 - x];
580             row[image->w - 1 - x] = temp;
581           }
582         }
583 #endif
584       }
585 
586       // Apply vertical flip (Y-axis)
587       if (flip_y_enabled) {
588         for (int y = 0; y < image->h / 2; y++) {
589           rgb_pixel_t *top_row = &flipped_image->pixels[y * image->w];
590           rgb_pixel_t *bottom_row = &flipped_image->pixels[(image->h - 1 - y) * image->w];
591           for (int x = 0; x < image->w; x++) {
592             rgb_pixel_t temp = top_row[x];
593             top_row[x] = bottom_row[x];
594             bottom_row[x] = temp;
595           }
596         }
597       }
598 
599       uint64_t t_flip_reverse_end = time_get_ns();
600       display_image = flipped_image;
601 
602       log_dev("TIMING_FLIP: alloc=%llu us, memcpy=%llu us, flip=%llu us (x=%d, y=%d)",
603               (t_flip_alloc_end - t_flip_alloc_start) / 1000, (t_flip_memcpy_end - t_flip_memcpy_start) / 1000,
604               (t_flip_reverse_end - t_flip_reverse_start) / 1000, flip_x_enabled, flip_y_enabled);
605     }
606     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 3 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "image_flip",
607                              "IMAGE_FLIP: Flip complete (%.2f ms)");
608   }
609   uint64_t t_flip_end = time_get_ns();
610 
611   uint64_t t_filter_start = time_get_ns();
612 
613   // Apply color filter to RGB pixels before ASCII conversion
614   // Rainbow filter is handled separately via ANSI color replacement
615   image_t *filtered_image = NULL;
616   if (color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE && color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
617     // Create a copy to avoid modifying the original
618     filtered_image = image_new((size_t)display_image->w, (size_t)display_image->h);
619     if (filtered_image) {
620       memcpy(filtered_image->pixels, display_image->pixels,
621              (size_t)display_image->w * (size_t)display_image->h * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t));
622 
623       // Apply filter to the copy
624       int stride = (int)display_image->w * 3;
625       float time_seconds = (float)t_filter_start / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
626       apply_color_filter((uint8_t *)filtered_image->pixels, (uint32_t)display_image->w, (uint32_t)display_image->h,
627                          (uint32_t)stride, color_filter, time_seconds);
628     }
629   }
630 
631   const image_t *ascii_input_image = filtered_image ? filtered_image : display_image;
632   uint64_t t_filter_end = time_get_ns();
633 
634   uint64_t t_convert_start = time_get_ns();
635   // Call the standard ASCII conversion using the filtered (or original) image
636   START_TIMER("ascii_convert_with_capabilities");
637   char *result = ascii_convert_with_capabilities(ascii_input_image, width, height, &caps_copy, preserve_aspect_ratio,
638                                                  stretch, ctx->palette_chars);
639   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "ascii_convert_with_capabilities",
640                            "ASCII_CONVERT: Conversion complete (%.2f ms)");
641   uint64_t t_convert_end = time_get_ns();
642 
643   // Apply rainbow color filter to ANSI output by replacing RGB values
644   // This preserves character selection while applying the filter colors
645   if (result && color_filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
646     uint64_t t_color_replace_start = time_get_ns();
647     float time_seconds = (float)t_filter_start / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
648 
649     char *rainbow_result = rainbow_replace_ansi_colors(result, time_seconds);
650     if (rainbow_result) {
651       SAFE_FREE(result);
652       result = rainbow_result;
653     }
654 
655     uint64_t t_color_replace_end = time_get_ns();
656     char color_replace_str[32];
657     time_pretty(t_color_replace_end - t_color_replace_start, -1, color_replace_str, sizeof(color_replace_str));
658     log_dev("COLOR_REPLACE: %s", color_replace_str);
659   }
660 
661   // Apply digital rain effect if enabled
662   if (result && ctx->digital_rain) {
663     uint64_t t_rain_start = time_get_ns();
664     uint64_t current_time_ns = t_rain_start;
665     float delta_time = (float)(current_time_ns - ctx->last_frame_time_ns) / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
666     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = current_time_ns;
667 
668     // Update digital rain color from current filter (allows live filter changes)
669     color_filter_t current_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
670     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, current_filter);
671 
672     char *rain_result = digital_rain_apply(ctx->digital_rain, result, delta_time);
673     if (rain_result) {
674       SAFE_FREE(result);
675       result = rain_result;
676     }
677 
678     uint64_t t_rain_end = time_get_ns();
679     char rain_str[32];
680     time_pretty(t_rain_end - t_rain_start, -1, rain_str, sizeof(rain_str));
681     log_dev("DIGITAL_RAIN: Effect applied (%s)", rain_str);
682   }
683 
684   uint64_t t_cleanup_start = time_get_ns();
685   // Clean up flipped and filtered images if created
686   START_TIMER("ascii_convert_cleanup");
687   if (filtered_image) {
688     image_destroy(filtered_image);
689   }
690   if (flipped_image) {
691     image_destroy(flipped_image);
692   }
693   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 2 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "ascii_convert_cleanup",
694                            "ASCII_CONVERT_CLEANUP: Cleanup complete (%.2f ms)");
695   uint64_t t_cleanup_end = time_get_ns();
696 
697   // Log total breakdown with actual measured times
698   log_dev("CONVERT_TIMING: flip=%llu us, filter=%llu us, convert=%llu us, cleanup=%llu us, TOTAL=%llu us",
699           (t_flip_end - t_flip_start) / 1000, (t_filter_end - t_filter_start) / 1000,
700           (t_convert_end - t_convert_start) / 1000, (t_cleanup_end - t_cleanup_start) / 1000,
701           (t_cleanup_end - t_flip_start) / 1000);
702 
703   return result;
704 }

session_display_ctx_t * session_display_create (const
  session_display_config_t * config)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Create a new session display context.


Parameters


config Display configuration (must not be
  NULL)


Returns


Pointer to display context, or NULL on failure


Creates and initializes a session display context with the
    specified configuration. Detects terminal capabilities and initializes
    palette.




Note


Call session_display_destroy() to free resources
  when done.



On failure, sets asciichat_errno with error details.




Create a new session display context.




Executes comprehensive terminal reset sequence for clean display
    state. Skips terminal control operations in snapshot mode.




Parameters


snapshot_mode Whether snapshot mode is
  enabled


Definition at line 131 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


131                                                                                       {
132   // Auto-create config from command-line options if NULL
133   session_display_config_t auto_config = {0};
134   if (!config) {
135     auto_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
136     auto_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
137     auto_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
138     auto_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
139     config = &auto_config;
140   }
141 
142   // Check if we should exit before starting initialization
143   if (config->should_exit_callback && config->should_exit_callback(config->callback_data)) {
144     return NULL;
145   }
146 
147   // Allocate context
148   session_display_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(session_display_ctx_t), session_display_ctx_t *);
149 
150   // Store configuration
151   ctx->snapshot_mode = config->snapshot_mode;
152   ctx->palette_type = config->palette_type;
153   ctx->audio_playback_enabled = config->enable_audio_playback;
154   ctx->audio_ctx = config->audio_ctx;
155   ctx->render_fps = config->render_fps;
156   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->first_frame, true);
157   atomic_store_bool(&ctx->keyboard_help_active, false);
158 
159   // Initialize FPS counter
160   ctx->fps_counter = fps_counter_create();
161 
162   // Get TTY info for direct terminal access
163   ctx->tty_info = get_current_tty();
164 
165   // Determine if we have a valid TTY
166   // Check if stdout is a TTY, not just any fd (stdin/stderr could be TTY while stdout is piped).
167   // If stdout is piped/redirected, never perform terminal operations regardless of other fds.
168   if (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
169     ctx->has_tty = (platform_isatty(ctx->tty_info.fd) != 0) && terminal_is_stdout_tty();
170   }
171 
172   // In piped mode, force all logs to stderr to prevent frame data corruption
173   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
174     log_set_force_stderr(true);
175     // Disable buffering for stdout to ensure frames appear immediately
176     // Each frame ends with newline, so unbuffered output is critical for real-time display
177     (void)setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
178   }
179 
180   // Also disable buffering for TTY mode to ensure smooth 40+ FPS animation
181   setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
182 
183   // Detect terminal capabilities
184   ctx->caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
185 
186   // Set wants_padding based on terminal output mode
187   // Enable padding for all rendering modes (including snapshot) to center output
188   // The padding adds newlines at the top, which is useful for visual centering
189   bool is_snapshot_mode = config->snapshot_mode;
190   ctx->caps.wants_padding = true;
191 
192   // Calculate pad_height: when padding is enabled, add top padding for centering
193   // Halfblock mode uses 2 source rows per output row, so padding is halved
194   if (ctx->caps.wants_padding) {
195     ctx->caps.pad_height = 5; // Halfblock needs half the padding (10 / 2 = 5)
196   } else {
197     ctx->caps.pad_height = 0;
198   }
199 
200   log_debug("Padding mode: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, has_tty=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d), pad_height=%zu",
201             ctx->caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, ctx->has_tty, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
202             ctx->caps.pad_height);
203 
204   // Apply color mode override if specified
205   if (config->color_mode != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
206     ctx->caps.color_level = config->color_mode;
207   }
208 
209   // Apply command-line overrides
210   ctx->caps = apply_color_mode_override(ctx->caps);
211 
212   // Initialize palette
213   int palette_result = initialize_client_palette(config->palette_type, config->custom_palette, ctx->palette_chars,
214                                                  &ctx->palette_len, ctx->luminance_palette);
215   if (palette_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
216     log_warn("Failed to initialize palette, using default");
217     // Fall back to standard palette
218     (void)initialize_client_palette(PALETTE_STANDARD, NULL, ctx->palette_chars, &ctx->palette_len,
219                                     ctx->luminance_palette);
220   }
221 
222   // Pre-warm UTF-8 palette cache during initialization (not during rendering)
223   // This is expensive on first use (creates lookup tables), so warm it up now to avoid frame lag
224   // This function is imported from lib/video/simd/common.h
225   (void)get_utf8_palette_cache((const char *)ctx->palette_chars);
226   log_debug("UTF-8 palette cache pre-warmed during display initialization");
227 
228   // Initialize ANSI fast lookup tables based on terminal capabilities
229   if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR) {
230     ansi_fast_init();
231   } else if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_256) {
232     ansi_fast_init_256color();
233   } else if (ctx->caps.color_level == TERM_COLOR_16) {
234     ansi_fast_init_16color();
235   }
236   // TERM_COLOR_MONO requires no init
237 
238   // Initialize ASCII output system if we have a TTY
239   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
240     ascii_write_init(ctx->tty_info.fd, false);
241   }
242 
243   // Initialize digital rain effect if enabled
244   if (GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
245     // Get terminal dimensions for grid size
246     unsigned short int width_us = terminal_get_effective_width();
247     unsigned short int height_us = terminal_get_effective_height();
248 
249     int width = (int)width_us;
250     int height = (int)height_us;
251 
252     ctx->digital_rain = digital_rain_init(width, height);
253     if (ctx->digital_rain) {
254       // Set color from color filter if active
255       color_filter_t filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
256       digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, filter);
257       log_info("Digital rain effect enabled: %dx%d grid", width, height);
258     } else {
259       log_warn("Failed to initialize digital rain effect");
260     }
261     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = time_get_ns();
262   }
263 
264 #ifndef _WIN32
265   // Initialize render-file if enabled (FFmpeg encodes to stdout when "-" is specified)
266   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
267   log_info("DISPLAY_CREATE: render-file opt='%s' (len=%zu), will initialize=%s",
268            render_file_opt ? render_file_opt : "(null)",
269            render_file_opt ? strlen(render_file_opt) : 0,
270            (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0) ? "YES" : "NO");
271   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0) {
272     int width = (int)GET_OPTION(width);
273     int height = (int)GET_OPTION(height);
274     log_info("render-file: Creating encoder with cols=%d, rows=%d", width, height);
275     // Determine encoder FPS: in snapshot mode, use actual capture rate for correct duration;
276     // in normal mode, use configured fps option
277     uint32_t encoder_fps = 0;
278     if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && config->render_fps > 0) {
279       // Snapshot mode with known capture rate - use capture rate for correct duration
280       encoder_fps = config->render_fps;
281       log_debug("render-file: SNAPSHOT MODE - using capture rate FPS=%u (snapshot_delay=%.2f sec)", encoder_fps,
282                 GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay));
283     } else if (config->render_fps > 0) {
284       // Normal mode with known capture rate - use it
285       encoder_fps = config->render_fps;
286       log_debug("render-file: Using capture rate FPS=%u for encoding", encoder_fps);
287     } else {
288       // No capture rate available - use option default
289       encoder_fps = (uint32_t)GET_OPTION(fps);
290       if (encoder_fps == 0)
291         encoder_fps = 60;
292       log_debug("render-file: Using option FPS=%u (no capture rate available)", encoder_fps);
293     }
294     log_info("render-file: Final encoder FPS=%u (config_render_fps=%u, option_fps=%u)", encoder_fps, config->render_fps,
295              (uint32_t)GET_OPTION(fps));
296     log_info("render-file: Calling render_file_create with path='%s', %dx%d, fps=%d",
297              render_file_opt, width, height, encoder_fps);
298     asciichat_error_t rf_err = render_file_create(render_file_opt, width, height, (int)encoder_fps,
299                                                   GET_OPTION(render_theme), &ctx->render_file);
300     if (rf_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
301       log_error("render-file: FAILED to create with error %d (ctx->render_file=%p)",
302                 rf_err, (void *)ctx->render_file);
303     } else {
304       log_info("render-file: SUCCESS - encoder initialized, ctx->render_file=%p", (void *)ctx->render_file);
305       // Set audio sources if render_file_set_audio_source is available
306       if (ctx->render_file && config->render_file_audio_source) {
307         render_file_set_audio_source((render_file_ctx_t *)ctx->render_file, config->render_file_audio_source, NULL);
308         log_debug("render-file: audio source set (media_source=%p)", config->render_file_audio_source);
309       }
310       if (ctx->render_file && config->render_file_audio_capture_rb) {
311         render_file_set_audio_source((render_file_ctx_t *)ctx->render_file, NULL, config->render_file_audio_capture_rb);
312         log_debug("render-file: audio capture ring buffer set");
313       }
314     }
315   } else {
316     log_info("render-file: NOT initializing (render_file_opt is %s)", render_file_opt ? "empty" : "NULL");
317   }
318 #endif
319 
320   ctx->initialized = true;
321   return ctx;
322 }

void
  session_display_destroy (session_display_ctx_t * ctx)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Destroy session display context and free resources.


Parameters


ctx Display context to destroy (can be NULL)


Cleans up the display context, restores terminal state, and
    releases all resources. Safe to call with NULL.


Definition at line 324 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


324                                                          {
325   if (!ctx) {
326     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Session display context is NULL");
327     return;
328   }
329 
330   // Cleanup ASCII rendering if we had a TTY
331   // Don't reset terminal in snapshot mode to preserve the rendered output
332   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
333     ascii_write_destroy(ctx->tty_info.fd, !ctx->snapshot_mode);
334   }
335 
336   // Close the controlling terminal if we opened it
337   if (ctx->tty_info.owns_fd && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
338     (void)platform_close(ctx->tty_info.fd);
339     ctx->tty_info.fd = -1;
340     ctx->tty_info.owns_fd = false;
341   }
342 
343   // Cleanup digital rain effect
344   if (ctx->digital_rain) {
345     digital_rain_destroy(ctx->digital_rain);
346     ctx->digital_rain = NULL;
347   }
348 
349   // Cleanup FPS counter
350   if (ctx->fps_counter) {
351     fps_counter_destroy(ctx->fps_counter);
352     ctx->fps_counter = NULL;
353   }
354 
355 #ifndef _WIN32
356   // Cleanup render-file if active
357   if (ctx->render_file) {
358     render_file_destroy(ctx->render_file);
359     ctx->render_file = NULL;
360   }
361 #endif
362 
363   ctx->initialized = false;
364 
365   // Clear the cached display context in splash state to prevent use-after-free
366   // when worker threads try to access the display after it's been freed
367   splash_clear_display_context();
368 
369   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
370 }

void
  session_display_render_fps_overlay (session_display_ctx_t *
  ctx)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Render FPS counter overlay in top-right corner.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


Displays the current FPS value as a reverse-video overlay in the
    top-right corner of the terminal. Only renders in TTY mode. Safe to call
    when fps_counter is NULL.


Definition at line 967 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


967                                                                     {
968   if (!ctx || !ctx->fps_counter || !ctx->initialized) {
969     return;
970   }
971 
972   // Only render overlay in TTY mode
973   if (!ctx->has_tty) {
974     return;
975   }
976 
977   // Get current FPS value from counter
978   float fps = fps_counter_get(ctx->fps_counter);
979 
980   // Get terminal width for right-alignment
981   int term_cols = (int)terminal_get_effective_width();
982 
983   // Position at row 1, right side of screen
984   // Column = term_cols - 7 (to fit "FPS:144" which is 7 chars)
985   // Use reverse video (\033[7m) for visibility, then reset (\033[0m)
986   char overlay[64];
987   int overlay_len = snprintf(overlay, sizeof(overlay), "\033[1;%dH\033[7mFPS:%3.0f\033[0m", term_cols - 6, fps);
988 
989   if (overlay_len > 0 && overlay_len < (int)sizeof(overlay)) {
990     // Write overlay to TTY
991     int fd = (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
992     (void)platform_write_all(fd, overlay, overlay_len);
993 
994     // Flush to ensure overlay appears immediately
995     (void)terminal_flush(fd);
996   }
997 }

void
  session_display_write_ascii (session_display_ctx_t * ctx, const char *
  frame_data)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Write ASCII frame to terminal only (no encoding).


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


frame_data ASCII frame data (must not be NULL)


Writes ANSI string to terminal/stdout without encoding to file.
    Used by the main thread in the threaded pipeline.


Definition at line 726 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


726                                                                                 {
727   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized) {
728     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Display context is NULL or uninitialized");
729     return;
730   }
731 
732   if (!ascii) {
733     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ASCII data is NULL");
734     return;
735   }
736 
737   // Re-evaluate color_level on every frame to pick up live color_mode changes
738   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
739   if (color_mode != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
740     ctx->caps.color_level = color_mode;
741   }
742 
743   // Suppress frame rendering when help screen is active
744   if (atomic_load_bool(&ctx->keyboard_help_active)) {
745     return;
746   }
747 
748   // Calculate frame length
749   size_t frame_len = strnlen(ascii, 1024 * 1024); // Max 1MB frame
750   if (frame_len == 0) {
751     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ASCII data is empty");
752     return;
753   }
754 
755   // Apply digital rain effect if enabled
756   char *display_frame = (char *)ascii;
757   char *rain_result = NULL;
758   if (ctx->digital_rain) {
759     uint64_t t_rain_start = time_get_ns();
760     uint64_t current_time_ns = t_rain_start;
761     float delta_time = (float)(current_time_ns - ctx->last_frame_time_ns) / (float)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
762     ctx->last_frame_time_ns = current_time_ns;
763 
764     // Update digital rain color from current filter
765     color_filter_t current_filter = GET_OPTION(color_filter);
766     digital_rain_set_color_from_filter(ctx->digital_rain, current_filter);
767 
768     rain_result = digital_rain_apply(ctx->digital_rain, (char *)ascii, delta_time);
769     if (rain_result) {
770       display_frame = rain_result;
771       frame_len = strnlen(rain_result, 1024 * 1024);
772 
773       uint64_t t_rain_end = time_get_ns();
774       char rain_str[32];
775       time_pretty(t_rain_end - t_rain_start, -1, rain_str, sizeof(rain_str));
776       log_info("DIGITAL_RAIN (write_ascii): Effect applied (%s)", rain_str);
777     }
778   }
779 
780   // Handle first frame - perform initial terminal reset and splash cleanup
781   bool was_first_frame = atomic_exchange_bool(&ctx->first_frame, false);
782   if (was_first_frame) {
783     // Stop splash screen when first frame is ready
784     splash_intro_done();
785 
786     // Perform initial terminal reset
787     if (ctx->has_tty && !ctx->render_file) {
788       (void)terminal_reset(STDOUT_FILENO);
789       (void)terminal_clear_screen();
790       (void)terminal_cursor_home(STDOUT_FILENO);
791       (void)terminal_clear_scrollback(STDOUT_FILENO);
792       (void)terminal_cursor_show();
793       if (!ctx->snapshot_mode) {
794         (void)terminal_cursor_hide();
795       }
796       (void)terminal_flush(STDOUT_FILENO);
797     }
798   }
799 
800   // Output routing logic
801   bool use_tty_control = ctx->has_tty && (!ctx->snapshot_mode || terminal_is_interactive());
802 
803   START_TIMER("frame_write");
804 
805   if (use_tty_control) {
806     // TTY mode: Buffer cursor control + frame data together for atomic frame display
807     const char *cursor_home_sequence = "\033[H\033[3J"; // 7 bytes total
808     size_t cursor_seq_len = 7;
809     size_t total_size = cursor_seq_len + frame_len;
810 
811     char *frame_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(total_size, char *);
812     if (frame_buffer) {
813       memcpy(frame_buffer, cursor_home_sequence, cursor_seq_len);
814       memcpy(frame_buffer + cursor_seq_len, display_frame, frame_len);
815 
816       log_debug("FRAME_WRITE_TTY: Writing %zu bytes (cursor=%zu + frame=%zu) to stdout", total_size, cursor_seq_len,
817                 frame_len);
818       ssize_t written = platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, frame_buffer, total_size);
819       log_debug("FRAME_WRITE_TTY: Wrote %zd bytes (requested %zu)", written, total_size);
820 
821       // Start snapshot timer on first ASCII frame rendered
822       if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
823         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
824         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
825         log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST ASCII FRAME RENDERED (write_ascii) - Timer started");
826       }
827 
828       // Tick FPS counter
829       if (ctx->fps_counter) {
830         fps_counter_tick(ctx->fps_counter);
831       }
832 
833       SAFE_FREE(frame_buffer);
834     }
835 
836     // Flush buffers
837     log_debug("FRAME_FLUSH: Flushing stdout");
838     (void)fflush(stdout);
839     int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
840     (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
841 
842     // Render FPS counter overlay if enabled
843     if (ctx->fps_counter && GET_OPTION(fps_counter)) {
844       session_display_render_fps_overlay(ctx);
845     }
846   } else if (terminal_is_interactive()) {
847     // Piped to interactive terminal: output ASCII frames with newline
848     char *write_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_len + 1, char *);
849     if (write_buf) {
850       memcpy(write_buf, display_frame, frame_len);
851       write_buf[frame_len] = '\n';
852       (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, write_buf, frame_len + 1);
853       SAFE_FREE(write_buf);
854     }
855 
856     // Flush buffers
857     (void)fflush(stdout);
858     int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
859     (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
860   } else {
861     // Non-interactive piped output
862     // BUT: Don't write ASCII frames to stdout if render_file is using stdout (--render-file="-")
863     if (!ctx->render_file) {
864       // No render-file, safe to write ASCII frames to stdout
865       char *write_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(frame_len + 1, char *);
866       if (write_buf) {
867         memcpy(write_buf, display_frame, frame_len);
868         write_buf[frame_len] = '\n';
869         (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, write_buf, frame_len + 1);
870 
871         // Start snapshot timer on first ASCII frame rendered
872         if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
873           g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
874           g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
875           log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST ASCII FRAME RENDERED (write_ascii piped) - Timer started");
876         }
877 
878         SAFE_FREE(write_buf);
879       }
880 
881       // Flush buffers (skip terminal_flush on pipes to avoid blocking in snapshot mode)
882       (void)fflush(stdout);
883       if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) || ctx->has_tty) {
884         int flush_fd = (ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) ? ctx->tty_info.fd : STDOUT_FILENO;
885         (void)terminal_flush(flush_fd);
886       }
887     } else {
888       // render_file is using stdout: skip ASCII output to avoid mixing with video
889       if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && !g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered) {
890         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered = true;
891         g_snapshot_first_frame_rendered_ns = time_get_ns();
892         log_info("SNAPSHOT: FIRST FRAME RENDERED (render_file piped) - Timer started");
893       }
894     }
895   }
896 
897   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "frame_write",
898                            "FRAME_WRITE: Write and flush complete (%.2f ms)");
899 
900   // Clean up digital rain result if allocated
901   if (rain_result) {
902     SAFE_FREE(rain_result);
903   }
904 }

asciichat_error_t
  session_display_write_audio (session_display_ctx_t * ctx, const float *
  buffer, size_t num_samples)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Write audio samples to playback buffer.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


buffer Audio samples to write (must not be NULL)


num_samples Number of samples to write


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Writes audio samples to the playback ring buffer for playback
    through speakers. Used in mirror mode to play file audio or other audio
    sources.


Definition at line 1056 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


1056                                                                                                                    {
1057   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !buffer || num_samples == 0) {
1058     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, buffer=%p, num_samples=%zu", ctx, buffer,
1059                      num_samples);
1060   }
1061 
1062   if (!ctx->audio_playback_enabled || !ctx->audio_ctx) {
1063     // Audio not enabled, but not an error - just skip silently
1064     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1065   }
1066 
1067   // For mirror mode with local files: write samples directly without jitter buffering
1068   // The jitter buffer is designed for network scenarios with irregular packet arrivals
1069   // For local playback, we just want raw samples flowing to the speakers
1070   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t *)ctx->audio_ctx;
1071   if (audio_ctx && audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
1072     audio_ring_buffer_t *rb = audio_ctx->playback_buffer;
1073 
1074     // Simple direct write: append samples to ring buffer without network-oriented complexity
1075     uint32_t write_idx = (uint32_t)atomic_load_u64(&rb->write_index);
1076     uint32_t read_idx = (uint32_t)atomic_load_u64(&rb->read_index);
1077 
1078     // Calculate available space in ring buffer
1079     uint32_t available = (read_idx - write_idx - 1) & (AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - 1);
1080 
1081     if ((uint32_t)num_samples > available) {
1082       // Buffer full - just skip this write to avoid distortion from overwriting old audio
1083       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1084     }
1085 
1086     // Direct memcpy to ring buffer, handling wrap-around
1087     uint32_t space_before_wrap = AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE - write_idx;
1088     if ((uint32_t)num_samples <= space_before_wrap) {
1089       memcpy(&rb->data[write_idx], buffer, num_samples * sizeof(float));
1090     } else {
1091       // Split write: first part to end of buffer, second part wraps to beginning
1092       uint32_t first_part = space_before_wrap;
1093       uint32_t second_part = num_samples - first_part;
1094       memcpy(&rb->data[write_idx], buffer, first_part * sizeof(float));
1095       memcpy(&rb->data[0], &buffer[first_part], second_part * sizeof(float));
1096     }
1097 
1098     // Update write index atomically
1099     atomic_store_u64(&rb->write_index, (write_idx + num_samples) % AUDIO_RING_BUFFER_SIZE);
1100 
1101     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1102   }
1103 
1104   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1105 }

    session_display_ctx::audio_ctx,
    session_display_ctx::audio_playback_enabled, and
    session_display_ctx::initialized.






void
  session_display_write_raw (session_display_ctx_t * ctx, const char *
  data, size_t len)


#include <src/common/session/display.h>


Render raw bytes to the terminal without frame processing.


Parameters


ctx Display context (must not be NULL)


data Raw byte data to write (must not be NULL)


len Length of data in bytes


Directly writes raw bytes to the terminal without any frame
    processing. Useful for RLE-expanded frames or pre-formatted output.


Definition at line 947 of file
    src/common/session/display.c.


947                                                                                          {
948   if (!ctx || !ctx->initialized || !data || len == 0) {
949     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: ctx=%p, data=%p, len=%zu", ctx, data, len);
950     return;
951   }
952 
953   int fd = -1;
954   if (ctx->has_tty && ctx->tty_info.fd >= 0) {
955     fd = ctx->tty_info.fd;
956   } else {
957     fd = STDOUT_FILENO;
958   }
959 
960   // Write all data with automatic retry on transient errors
961   (void)platform_write_all(fd, data, len);
962 
963   // Flush immediately after write to TTY to ensure data is sent
964   (void)terminal_flush(fd);
965 }

Referenced by keyboard_help_render().






void
  session_handle_keyboard_input (session_capture_ctx_t * capture,
  session_display_ctx_t * display, keyboard_key_t key)


#include
    <src/common/session/keyboard_handler.h>


Handle keyboard input in a session.


Parameters


capture Capture context (may be NULL for
  client-only mode)


display Display context (may be NULL if not needed)


key Keyboard key code from keyboard_key_t enumeration


Processes keyboard input and performs appropriate session actions.
    Supports both mirror mode (local capture) and client mode (network
    rendering).




Keyboard Controls:


    
  
  • '?' key: Toggle help screen on/off (all modes)
    • 
  
  • Left Arrow: Seek -30 seconds (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • Right Arrow: Seek +30 seconds (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • Up Arrow: Increase volume +10% (all modes with audio)
    • 
  
  • Down Arrow: Decrease volume -10% (all modes with audio)
    • 
  
  • Spacebar: Play/pause toggle (file/URL sources only)
    • 
  
  • 'c' key: Cycle through color modes (mono → 16 → 256
      → truecolor)
    • 
  
  • 'm' key: Toggle mute (remembers previous volume for unmute)
    • 
  
  • 'x' key: Flip webcam horizontally (mirroring)
    • 
  
  • 'y' key: Flip webcam vertically
    • 



Source-specific behavior:


    
  
  • Help screen: Works in all modes (toggles with '?')
    • 
  
  • Seek/pause controls: Only work with file and URL sources (not webcam)
    • 
  
  • Volume/mute: Work with any audio-capable source
    • 
  
  • Color cycling: Works with all rendering modes
    • 
  
  • Flip: Works with all capture sources (webcam, media files, etc.)
    • 



Audio volume:


    
  
  • Volume range: [0.0, 1.0] (0% = silent, 100% = maximum/normal)
    • 
  
  • Default volume: 1.0 (100%)
    • 
  
  • Mute state: Remembers the previous non-zero volume when toggled back
    on
    • 
  
  • Unrecognized keys are silently ignored (no error returned)
    • 



Thread safety:


    
  
  • Safe to call from render thread (video/audio threads)
    • 
  
  • Safe to call concurrently from multiple render threads
    • 
  
  • Uses RCU-protected options for atomic updates
    • 
  
  • No explicit locking required (option system handles synchronization)
    • 
  
  • Does not block waiting for I/O or locks
    • 



Error handling:


    
  
  • Always succeeds (void return)
    • 
  
  • Invalid or unrecognized keys are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • If capture is NULL (client-only mode), seek/pause requests are silently
      ignored
    • 
  
  • If display is NULL (non-TTY mode), help screen toggle is silently
    ignored
    • 
  
  • If audio system is unavailable, volume changes are silently ignored
    • 



Parameters


capture NULL for client-only mode (network
  rendering), non-NULL for local capture


display Display context for help screen toggle (may be NULL)


key Valid key code from keyboard_key_t enum (0-127 for ASCII, special key
  codes)


Note


Safe to call without prior keyboard_init() (silently
  handles invalid keys)



In client-only mode, capture is NULL (seek/pause unavailable,
    other controls work)




Volume adjustments are immediate without audible artifacts




Mute persists across multiple mute toggles with volume memory

  keyboard_handler.c.


87                                                                                                                        {
88   // Debug: log all key codes to help identify unknown keys
89   if (key != KEY_NONE) {
90     log_info("KEYBOARD INPUT: code=%d (0x%02x) char='%c'", key, key, (key >= 32 && key < 127) ? key : '?');
91     if (key == 'x' || key == 'X') {
92       log_info("!!! X KEY DETECTED AT HANDLER ENTRY !!!");
93     }
94   }
95 
96   switch ((int)key) {
97   // ===== HELP SCREEN TOGGLE =====
98   case KEY_QUESTION: {
99     log_info("✓✓✓ USER PRESSED ? KEY - HELP SCREEN TOGGLE ✓✓✓");
100     if (display) {
101       log_info("Toggling help screen (display=%p)", (void *)display);
102       keyboard_help_toggle(display);
103       // Render help screen immediately so user sees it
104       keyboard_help_render(display);
105       log_info("✓ Help screen toggle complete");
106     } else {
107       log_error("ERROR: Cannot toggle help - display context is NULL");
108     }
109     break;
110   }
111 
112   // ===== HELP SCREEN CLOSE / QUIT =====
113   case KEY_ESCAPE: {
114     if (display && keyboard_help_is_active(display)) {
115       // Close help screen if it's active
116       keyboard_help_toggle(display);
117       terminal_clear_screen();
118     } else {
119       // If help screen is not active, quit the app (like Ctrl-C)

121       raise(SIGINT);
122     }
123     break;
124   }
125 
126   // ===== SEEK CONTROLS (file sources only) =====
127   case KEY_LEFT: { // Seek backward 30 seconds
128     if (capture) {
129       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
130       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
131         double current_pos = media_source_get_position(source);
132         if (current_pos >= 0.0) {
133           double new_pos = current_pos - 30.0;
134           if (new_pos < 0.0) {
135             new_pos = 0.0;
136           }
137           asciichat_error_t err = media_source_seek(source, new_pos);
138           if (err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
139             log_info("Seeked backward to %.1f seconds", new_pos);
140           }
141           void *audio_ctx = session_capture_get_audio_context(capture);
142           if (audio_ctx) {
143             audio_flush_playback_buffers((audio_context_t *)audio_ctx);
144           }
145         }
146       }
147     }
148     break;
149   }
150 
151   case KEY_RIGHT: { // Seek forward 30 seconds
152     if (capture) {
153       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
154       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
155         double current_pos = media_source_get_position(source);
156         double duration = media_source_get_duration(source);
157         if (current_pos >= 0.0) {
158           double new_pos = current_pos + 30.0;
159           // Clamp to duration if known
160           if (duration > 0.0 && new_pos > duration) {
161             new_pos = duration;
162           }
163           asciichat_error_t err = media_source_seek(source, new_pos);
164           if (err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
165             log_info("Seeked forward to %.1f seconds", new_pos);
166           }
167           void *audio_ctx = session_capture_get_audio_context(capture);
168           if (audio_ctx) {
169             audio_flush_playback_buffers((audio_context_t *)audio_ctx);
170           }
171         }
172       }
173     }
174     break;
175   }
176 
177   // ===== VOLUME CONTROLS =====
178   case KEY_DOWN: { // Decrease volume 10%
179     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
180     double new_volume = clamp_volume(current_volume - 0.1);
181     options_set_double("speakers_volume", new_volume);
182     double verify_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
183     log_info("Volume DOWN: %.0f%% → %.0f%% (verified: %.0f%%)", current_volume * 100.0, new_volume * 100.0,
184              verify_volume * 100.0);
185     break;
186   }
187 
188   case KEY_UP: { // Increase volume 10%
189     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
190     double new_volume = clamp_volume(current_volume + 0.1);
191     options_set_double("speakers_volume", new_volume);
192     double verify_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
193     log_info("Volume UP: %.0f%% → %.0f%% (verified: %.0f%%)", current_volume * 100.0, new_volume * 100.0,
194              verify_volume * 100.0);
195     break;
196   }
197 
198   // ===== PLAY/PAUSE CONTROL =====
199   case KEY_SPACE: { // Toggle play/pause
200     if (capture) {
201       media_source_t *source = (media_source_t *)session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
202       if (source && media_source_get_type(source) == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE) {
203         media_source_toggle_pause(source);
204         if (media_source_is_paused(source)) {
205           log_info("Paused");
206         } else {
207           log_info("Playing");
208         }
209       }
210     }
211     break;
212   }
213 
214   // ===== COLOR MODE CONTROL =====
215   case KEY_C:
216   case 'C': {
217     int current_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(color_mode);
218     int next_mode = next_color_mode(current_mode);
219     options_set_int("color_mode", next_mode);
220 
221     const char *mode_names[] = {"Mono", "16-color", "256-color", "Truecolor"};
222     if (next_mode >= 0 && next_mode <= 3) {
223       log_info("Color mode: %s", mode_names[next_mode]);
224     }
225     break;
226   }
227 
228   // ===== MUTE CONTROL =====
229   case KEY_M:
230   case 'M': {
231     double current_volume = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
232     log_debug("Mute toggle: current_volume=%.2f, g_mute_saved_volume=%.2f, threshold=0.01", current_volume,
233               g_mute_saved_volume);
234 
235     if (current_volume > 0.01) { // If not already muted
236       // Save current volume and mute
237       g_mute_saved_volume = current_volume;
238       options_set_double("speakers_volume", 0.0);
239       double verify = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
240       log_info("Muted: saved %.0f%%, set to 0%% (verified: %.2f)", g_mute_saved_volume * 100.0, verify);
241     } else {
242       // Restore previous volume
243       double restore_volume = g_mute_saved_volume > 0.0 ? g_mute_saved_volume : 0.5;
244       options_set_double("speakers_volume", restore_volume);
245       double verify = GET_OPTION(speakers_volume);
246       log_info("Unmuted: restored %.0f%% (verified: %.2f)", restore_volume * 100.0, verify);
247     }
248     break;
249   }
250 
251   // ===== RENDER MODE CONTROL =====
252   case KEY_R:
253   case 'R': {
254     int current_mode = (int)GET_OPTION(render_mode);
255     int next_mode = next_render_mode(current_mode);
256     options_set_int("render_mode", next_mode);
257 
258     const char *mode_names[] = {"Foreground", "Background", "Half-block"};
259     if (next_mode >= 0 && next_mode <= 2) {
260       log_info("Render mode: %s", mode_names[next_mode]);
261     }
262     break;
263   }
264 
265   // ===== COLOR FILTER CONTROL =====
266   case KEY_F:
267   case 'F': {
268     int current_filter = (int)GET_OPTION(color_filter);

271

273                                   "Teal", "Cyan",  "Pink",  "Red",   "Yellow",  "Rainbow"};
274     if (next_filter >= 0 && next_filter < (int)COLOR_FILTER_COUNT) {
275       log_info("Color filter: %s", filter_names[next_filter]);
276     }
277     break;
278   }
279 
280   // ===== HORIZONTAL FLIP CONTROL =====
281   case 'X':
282   case 'x': {
283     log_info("✓ X KEY PRESSED - handling horizontal flip");
284     bool current_flip_x = (bool)GET_OPTION(flip_x);
285     options_set_bool("flip_x", !current_flip_x);
286     log_info("Horizontal flip: %s (was: %s)", !current_flip_x ? "enabled" : "disabled",
287              current_flip_x ? "enabled" : "disabled");
288     break;
289   }
290 
291   // ===== VERTICAL FLIP CONTROL =====

293   case 'y': {

295     options_set_bool("flip_y", !current_flip_y);

306   }
307
